creator: sjg@juniper.net
text:
If you look at the patch in freefall.freebsd.org:~sjg/ports2bmake.tar.gz
you'll see that the dozen or so lines in bsd.port.mk that deal with man
pages, are replaced with a couple of inline loops for bmake (reproduced
below).

This is because bmake handles the iterator variables quite differently,
so all the escaping etc done in those .for loops does not work.

>    bapt@ pointed me to this commit on github:
>https://github.com/jrmarino/DPorts/commit/6c4e33ddfebc9645b63e3296e7a03f118cf7
>7e10
>which will hopefully fix the more generic issue.

Getting rid of $$$$$$$${__lang} will be an improvement.
but it may still be better to use the simpler inline loops for bmake:

Index: Mk/bsd.port.mk
===================================================================
--- Mk/bsd.port.mk	(revision 304584)
+++ Mk/bsd.port.mk	(working copy)
@@ -1143,6 +1143,20 @@
@@ -2990,6 +3004,14 @@
 .endif
 
 .if (defined(MLINKS) || defined(_MLINKS_PREPEND)) && !defined(_MLINKS)
+
+.if defined(.PARSEDIR)
+# inline loops are simpler
+_MLINKS=    ${_MLINKS_PREPEND} \
+	${MANLANG:S,^,man/,:S,/"",,:@m@${MLINKS:@p@${MAN${p:E}PREFIX}/$m/man${p:E}/$p${MANEXT}@}@}
+
+.else
+
 __pmlinks!=	${ECHO_CMD} '${MLINKS:S/	/ /}' | ${AWK} \
  '{ if (NF % 2 != 0) { print "broken"; exit; } \
 	for (i=1; i<=NF; i++) { \
@@ -3016,6 +3038,7 @@
 .endfor
 .endfor
 .endif
+.endif
 _COUNT=0
 .for ___tpmlinks in ${_MLINKS}
 .if ${_COUNT} == "1"
